campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary rule about development and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of interim development ordinance #2326
Bicycle bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for pedestrian and bicycle planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide which community organizations and events would receive grant money from the city. They approved funding for specific groups that applied for grants, set aside money for competitive event grants this fiscal year, and made some clarifications to the rules for how community groups can apply for event funding and street closures.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive secured grant funding
Allocated budget for competitive event grants for the fiscal year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grant applications
Determined individual dollar amounts for approved funding recipients

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve meeting minutes and hear public comments. Council members and staff discussed various committee reports, and the group reviewed agendas for upcoming board and commission meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling for boards and commissions meetings

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community programs in Sunnyvale. The meeting included approval of previous meeting notes and presentations from organizations seeking this funding to support local residents.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding decisions
Affordable housing and human services funding priorities
Approval of February meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on supporting local businesses through several initiatives, including a program to help businesses catch up on unpaid licenses without penalties, outreach efforts to get more shops involved in city programs, and campaigns to encourage residents to shop locally. The council also discussed how the city can partner with private companies to sponsor events and offer incentives to help retail stores, small businesses, and startups succeed in Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get current on licenses
Shopping center outreach and participation in city business programs
Summer Shop Local campaign to boost local retailers
Public-private partnerships for funding city events
Economic incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed proposals related to historic buildings and landmarks in the area. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing held
Hybrid meeting format - both in-person and Zoom participation available
Public comment deadline: 12 p.m. day of meeting via email
Review of historic properties and landmark designations

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine business items. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract to buy email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency disaster facility agreement with Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
